select_when: A public, ego-identified commitment has been made and subsequent evidence challenges it — the risk is motivated belief revision rather than honest updating.
A team is deep into a failing initiative and contrary evidence is being reframed, minimized, or explained away rather than genuinely weighted.
A decision-maker's stated reasoning is suspiciously well-aligned with the conclusion that protects their prior commitment.
Evidence avoidance is occurring — feedback mechanisms, review channels, or external assessments are being deprioritized without explicit justification.
Post-hoc rationalization is being offered as the original reasoning — the justification was constructed after the commitment, not before.
avoid_when: Genuine honest reconsideration has occurred and the position update is evidence-driven rather than dissonance-motivated — the model should not pathologize real belief updating.
The model is being used to dismiss any position-maintenance as rationalization without examining whether the evidence genuinely warrants the maintained position.
output_type: motivated belief revision detection and escalation warning

curation_notes.summary: Cognitive dissonance curated as a commitment-defense detection model — activates when the psychological machinery for dissonance reduction is distorting evidence processing rather than genuine belief updating occurring.
open_questions: The boundary between legitimate position maintenance and dissonance-motivated rationalization requires explicit evidence quality assessment — the model flags the risk, not the verdict.

select_when.note: Source is explicit about public commitment, ego investment, and evidence avoidance as the primary dissonance activation contexts — select_when maps directly to Festinger's commitment-dissonance mechanism.

avoid_when.note: Avoid_when conditions are reviewed normalizations to prevent the model from pathologizing all position consistency as rationalization.

input_type.note: Compact label for the committed-position-under-pressure situation that activates the dissonance mechanism.

output_type.note: Model flags the motivated-revision risk and escalation pattern — it does not resolve the question of whether the position should be maintained.

reasoning_types.note: Diagnostic — identifying dissonance reduction in action; metacognitive — examining the source of one's own certainty; causal — tracing the belief revision back to the commitment mechanism.
